title: Today's Tech Brief: Marketing demanded IT add website feature that was already working
summary: A look at marketing's IT requests, and a surprising website feature repeat

body.text: According to The Register, the marketing team made the request without realizing the feature was already implemented. I am guessing the IT team handled it with amazing speed - and a hint of 'have you guys been checking the website?' - getting it done swiftly. A techie involved apparently regretted not taking credit for the speedy delivery.

What stands out to me here is the communication gap between departments. My read is that it's not just about IT and marketing - it's about how we all assume what others know or don't know. I think we've all been there at some point, making requests without having all the information. And then there's another story about a major tech firm facing a huge data breach. It's a reminder that even with all the tech in the world, communication and human error can still cause major issues.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.

sources.title: Marketing demanded IT add website feature that was already working
